Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)

 
 
Q4 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Which of the following static V-I characteristics represents a Bilateral device?

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
When the same relation exists between voltage and current for the current flowing in both directions or in other words, if the VI characteristics curve is similar in opposite quadrants then the element is bidirectional (or) bilateral. Hence, option (d) is correct.

Q5 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

To analyze a complex network which consists of a supernode, which of the following laws are required to write the supernodal equations?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
When only an ideal voltage source is connected between two non-reference nodes without any impedance, we form a supernode encompassing these two nodes for applying KCL while the voltage between the nodes are related by KVL. Thus, supernodal equations require applications of KCL, KVL and Ohm’s law.
